hypothesis: Matched architecture probe on lowrank_asymmetric_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual with K=128, EF=2, OPTIMIZER=signum, LR=3.0e-3 unchanged. Final reconstruction remains x_hat = U(Vx) + Σ_m pi_m c_m + Σ_i alpha_i^(1) b_i + Σ_j alpha_j^(2) b_j, where U(Vx) is a static low-rank trunk library, {c_m} is a static codebook library, and {b_i} is a static residual dictionary; only the stage-1/stage-2 budget split changes to a front-loaded 75/25 allocation. This is 扩展兼容 and exports cleanly to LUTurbo as trunk lookup + codebook lookup + two residual sparse lookups. Because this variant reuses the parent encoder modules, its selection-cost profile should stay matched to the mainline EF=2 point instead of introducing extra router/bucket scorer cost; the probe asks whether front-loading support budget improves low-EF reconstruction enough to justify future tuning.
next_hypotheses: If this probe is clearly worse in FVU or runtime, return directly to the mainline lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual recipe next round.
If it is competitive at similar runtime, the next single-variable follow-up is STAGE1_RATIO tuning within this asymmetric family rather than opening another new architecture family.
notes_to_memory: Compatibility: 扩展兼容. Export formula: x_hat = U(Vx) + Σ_m pi_m c_m + Σ_i alpha_i^(1) b_i + Σ_j alpha_j^(2) b_j.

Probe choice rationale: matched to the current mainline formula class and likely budget-safe at EF=2, unlike routed/bucketed matched probes which add extra selector layers and risk policy rejection on selection cost.
This round is intentionally only an architecture probe; K=128, EF=2, OPTIMIZER=signum, LR=3.0e-3 stay fixed.
self_review: Upside is bounded because this family only changes a fixed stage split and was not strong historically at higher-EF regimes, so a null result is plausible. Still, it is the cleanest policy-safe matched probe that isolates architecture without confounding cost increases.
summary: Run one matched architecture probe with ARCHITECTURE=lowrank_asymmetric_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual and keep the mainline recipe otherwise unchanged. It preserves LUTurbo-exportable static-library reconstruction and mainline-like selection cost, so the result will answer whether asymmetric budget allocation is worth any continued attention in the current low-EF regime.
